pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Compete and win mens physique in July 2018. Physical rolemodel for
kids and for my age group. Stay muscular with low bodyfat

Do you have any muscle groups you do nót want to get bigger?

No

Will you be performing any other form of exercise alongside
strength training (e.g. soccer, yoga, Olympic Weightlifting)?

Yoga

On the following 7-point scale, what's your dedication level?
1: I want some results for the minimum amount of effort.
4: I want to strike a reasonable balance between good results in
proportion to effort and sustainability.
7: I will do whatever it takes to achieve maximum results.
As of now 8sept-dec) due to kids 5, from jan 1st 7

How often in a week would you be prepared to train for maximal
results?
September-december due to travelling 3 times a week. Jan-July as often
as neccessary

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Misc.
What is your occupation?
You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal trainer/manual
labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm, stress and
activity level.
CEO

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had.

Bad back history but no problems now. Painful left shoulder, probably
rotator cuff (maybe torn)

Do you insist on any special diet (e.g. ketogenic, vegan)?

No

Lifestyle
Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train?
This includes when you're at work. If you don't answer this question
accurately with detailed times, you may get a program you can't follow(!)

I am able to rain every morning form 9-11 AM

BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Which of the following options best describes your activity level?
• Sedentary (e.g. office job)
x

• Somewhat active (e.g. you walk your dog several times a day or
you commute by bicycle)

• Active (e.g. full-time PT, literally on your feet most of the day)

• Very active (e.g. involved in manual labor)

Which of the following options best describes your stress level?
• Stress-free (e.g. on holiday)

• Only occasional/mild stress (e.g. student not during exam period)

• Average stress (e.g. full-time work with deadlines and commuting) x
• High stress (e.g. very high-paced work environment with great
responsibility)

How is your sleep rhythm and quality?

Good now, have been bad

How much caffeine do you consume daily on average or on a
typical work day?

0-1 cup

[Women only] Do you have a regular menstrual cycle? And are you
using any form of contraception? If these questions are sensitive, feel

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Usually 4-6 meals a day. 30P 50 CHO, 20 F. Have had best results
bodyfat wise with low fat diets, for muscle build red meat.

Please describe or attach your current training program in detail.

Everything form 3 full body to 5 split due to "program hookery"☺
Looking for program to follow consistently.

Please return completed form to Coaching@BayesianBodybuilding.com
and attach at least two full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting
(without excessive shadows; not your most flattering Facebook selfies),
preferably including a back double biceps and front relaxed pose.
If you recently had blood work done, please attach its results as well.
